deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1, Turn 3; Transcript 2, Turn 5; Transcript 3, Turn 8
deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—no rationale is offered for the Socratic approach or why the student should derive conclusions themselves. The tutoring constraints are enforced but never justified to the student.

In Transcript 1, after the student makes a good observation about the climate action plan (Turn 9), the tutor moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking. In Transcript 3, after the student successfully drafts a full actor classification (Turn 9), the tutor does not pause to ask what made the student's reasoning stronger compared to earlier attempts.

The tutor says 'Using my own judgment—not your instructor's—that's a solid structure,' which uses phrasing that mimics evaluative grading language ('solid structure') and could be read as a quasi-official assessment, even though the disclaimer is present. However, the disclaimer partially mitigates this. Deduction applied for borderline evaluative framing.

grade.overview: The tutor performs strongly across all three transcripts in maintaining Socratic method, avoiding direct answers, scaffolding well, and keeping key idea generation student-led. No submission-ready content was provided. The main weaknesses are: (1) the tutoring rationale is never explained to students—constraints are enforced but never justified; (2) reflection after student improvement is largely absent; (3) one instance of borderline evaluative framing in Transcript 3. The 3.2.B.a deduction is applied cautiously given the partial disclaimer present.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 receives full marks: the tutor consistently uses probing questions, avoids giving final answers, and keeps reasoning student-led across all 30 turns. 1.2 receives full marks: the tutor builds on student progress at each step and diagnoses confusion before responding. 1.3 loses 4 points: the tutoring rationale is never explained (1.3.A.a), and after student improvement moments the tutor moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed (1.3.C.a). Dialogue quality is clean with no repetition or off-topic drift. Communication is bite-sized and clear. The 3.2.B.a deduction is applied for the 'Using my own judgment' phrasing in Transcript 3 Turn 9, which edges toward evaluative framing despite the partial disclaimer.
